title: Foxborough files counterclaim to Patriots, Kraft Group’s lawsuit over annual stadium licensing fees
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Foxborough filed a 61-page counterclaim against Kraft Sports & Entertainment over annual licensing fees for Gillette Stadium.
The town argues Kraft Sports must cover public safety costs for stadium events, not local taxpayers.
The legal dispute involves $1 million in new financial obligations tied to the stadium's 2026 entertainment license.

title: UMass Lowell soccer player killed in Ohio car crash
author: Audrey Cooney
bullets: UMass Lowell soccer player Tobias Forsythe died in a car crash in Ohio on July 5.
The truck driver, Asrarov Bekhzod, is charged with tampering with evidence after the crash.
Forsythe, 21, studied economics and was remembered as a passionate athlete and cherished teammate.

title: Co-owner of Maine lumber mill dies weeks after fire, explosion that killed 2 others
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Alden J. Robbins, co-owner of Robbins Lumber, died from injuries sustained in a May 15 explosion and fire.
The explosion at Robbins Lumber injured 12 others and previously killed a firefighter and an assistant fire chief.
The cause of the explosion at the Maine lumber mill is under investigation, with rapid ignition cited as a factor.

title: Plymouth DA seeks assault and battery charge against Brockton mayor
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Plymouth County DA filed for a clerk-magistrate hearing to charge Brockton Mayor Moises Rodrigues with assault and battery.
The charge stems from allegations by a 17-year-old student accusing Mayor Rodrigues of inappropriate touching during a parade.
A previous harassment prevention order against Rodrigues was not extended, but his actions were called 'unwelcome' by a judge.

title: Police searching for tandem moped riders after shots fired in Roxbury
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Boston police seek public help to locate two suspects involved in a Roxbury shooting on June 30.
The suspects were last seen riding a moped in tandem down Schuyler Street towards Elm Hill Avenue.
Police released photos of the suspects, who wore black masks, and urged tips at (617) 343-4275.

title: Jaylen Brown comforts 6-year-old fan whose reaction to trade went viral
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jaylen Brown comforted six-year-old fan Giovanni Jean, upset over Brown's trade to the 76ers, during a meeting at MIT.
Jean's emotional reaction to the trade went viral, garnering over 1.1 million likes on Instagram.
Brown and Jean met at Brown's educational program, where Jean handed Brown a letter and received a hug.

title: Fatal Cambridge shooting renews criticism of council vote to end ShotSpotter contract
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Xavier Bautista was shot and killed in Cambridge early on July 4, with his body found nearly an hour later.
The Cambridge City Council ended the use of ShotSpotter technology, sparking debate after Bautista's death.
Cambridge officials acknowledged ShotSpotter could have reduced response time, as the shooting wasn't immediately reported to 911.

title: Jaylen Brown on Celtics trade: Boston is home and no organization can take that away
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jaylen Brown traded from the Boston Celtics to the Philadelphia 76ers but continues his community projects in Boston.
Governor Maura Healey declared July 7 as 7uice Foundation Day in Massachusetts to honor Brown's impact.
Brown made the NBA All-Star team five times with the Celtics and plans to continue his philanthropy in Boston.

title: Wrong-way I-95 driver faces drunk driving charges after crash, police say
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: John Tarshi, 25, of Lawrence faces drunk driving charges after crashing on I-95 near Exit 54A in Woburn.
Tarshi allegedly drove a Porsche the wrong way on the highway and refused field sobriety and breathalyzer tests.
A forged New Hampshire RMV document was found in Tarshi's car, and further charges may be pending.

title: All the new movies and TV shows streaming now (July 2026)
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Jennifer Garner stars in 'The Five-Star Weekend' on Peacock July 9, filmed on Nantucket with a notable ensemble cast.
HBO Max celebrates Shark Week with new shark content starting July 26, including the addition of 'Jaws' on Peacock.
HBO Max hosts the release of the Boston-filmed 'The Drama' on July 31, featuring highlights of new July releases.
